title: Minimum Number of Swaps to Make Palindromic Substrings Equal

statement.markdown: Given a string s and an integer k, return the minimum number of swaps required to make all substrings of s palindromic. Two substrings a and b are considered equal if they consist of exactly the same characters, not necessarily in the same order. The same character can be repeated any number of times.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be in the following format: s (string), k (integer)
statement.outputFormat: The output will be in the following format: an integer
statement.notes: The size of the string s will not exceed 1000. The number of distinct characters in the string s will not exceed 26.

constraints.description: The length of the string s
The maximum number of distinct characters allowed in a substring

examples.explanation: The string is already a palindrome, so no swaps are needed
The string is already a palindrome, so no swaps are needed

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
